headline: Sellers Dominate Friday Session as Surgical-Robot Rout Drags Healthcare Lower
body_html:
Equities are under broad pressure at midday Friday, with decliners outnumbering advancers by nearly two to one — 295 names lower against 157 higher across a 456-stock universe. The dominant tone is defensive retreat, driven in part by escalating tensions in the Middle East after the United States completed a sixth consecutive night of strikes on Iran, with Strait of Hormuz transit volumes falling sharply on the news. Safe-haven demand is pulling capital away from growth-sensitive sectors and into more defensive corners of the market.

Semiconductors and homebuilders lead the damage, off 1.4% and 1.24% respectively, while software, healthcare, and industrials each surrender between half a point and two-thirds of a point. Energy is the sole sector in the green, advancing 0.36% as Gulf supply anxiety keeps crude bids firm. The standout mover of the session is Intuitive Surgical, cratering 12.69% and dragging the broader healthcare-devices space lower. Electronic-design automation names Synopsys and Cadence Design are also sharply lower, off 10.61% and 9.82%.

On the upside, Insteel Industries leads all gainers with a 5.21% advance, followed by Dutch Bros at 4.79%. Into the close, traders will be watching whether energy can hold its gains and whether the software-EDA selloff deepens — options activity around large-cap technology names has been flagging unusually elevated expectations for near-term volatility heading into earnings season.

briefs.headline: Intuitive Surgical Sinks, Lands Session's Biggest Loss
briefs.body: Intuitive Surgical is down 12.69% at midday, making it the sharpest decliner in the universe by a wide margin. The move is pressuring the broader healthcare-devices group and contributing to a 0.59% sector-wide retreat. No other name in the session comes close to matching the magnitude of the drop.

briefs.headline: EDA Software Names Hit in Unusual Double Wipeout
briefs.body: Synopsys and Cadence Design are falling in tandem, losing 10.61% and 9.82% respectively, in what amounts to a sector-specific rout for electronic-design automation software. The simultaneous decline in both names is amplifying the broader software sector's 0.55% midday loss and drawing attention to the group's vulnerability ahead of earnings.

briefs.headline: Insteel Leads All Gainers With Steel-Driven Surge
briefs.body: Insteel Industries is up 5.21%, the best performance in the universe through midday, with the steel-products maker benefiting from materials demand optimism. The advance stands out against a broadly negative tape and helps limit the Materials sector's overall loss to 0.23%.

briefs.headline: Strait of Hormuz Pressure Lifts Energy Stocks
briefs.body: Energy is the only sector posting gains Friday, up 0.36%, as reports of declining tanker transits through the Strait of Hormuz and a suspected pirate seizure of a vessel in the Gulf of Aden stoke supply-disruption anxiety. The geopolitical premium in crude prices is lending support to energy names even as the rest of the market retreats.

briefs.headline: Gold on Track for Steepest Weekly Fall in Six Sessions
briefs.body: Bullion is heading for its largest weekly percentage decline in roughly six weeks, pressured by renewed inflation concerns and rising expectations that interest rates could move higher. The reversal is notable given gold's earlier safe-haven bids tied to Middle East tensions, suggesting rate sensitivity is currently overriding geopolitical support.

briefs.headline: Dutch Bros Bucks the Selloff With Nearly 5% Rally
briefs.body: Dutch Bros shares are up 4.79% at midday, one of the few consumer-sector bright spots on an otherwise difficult Friday. The drive-through coffee chain's advance is bucking a 0.39% consumer-sector decline and placing it second only to Insteel among the session's top performers.
